info.title: AIPOM Orchestrator API
info.description: RESTful API for the AIPOM AI project orchestrator. Exposes plan lifecycle, goal/task management, agent registry, spec validation, and a real-time SSE event stream.

get.description: Returns the full plan read-model including phases, brief, and history. Returns an empty discovery-stage plan when no plan file exists yet.
Returns the ordered history log of all plan state transitions.

post.description: Operator approves the discovery brief, advancing the plan from `discovery` to `architecture` status.

409.description: Plan is not in `discovery` status. The body reports the current vs expected status.

post.description: Launches the autonomous architecture planner in a background thread while the plan is in `architecture` status. Normally drafting auto-starts on `POST /plan/approve-brief`; this endpoint is the manual retry. The planner drafts the phase plan and decisions; each is published over SSE as `plan.decision_proposed` / `plan.phase_proposed`, and the session emits `plan.architecture_completed` / `plan.architecture_failed` on termination. Poll `GET /plan/architecture/status` for reload-safe readiness.

409.description: An architecture session is already in progress.

get.description: Reload-resilient readiness of the autonomous architecture run. `state` is `running` while drafting, `completed` once a usable roadmap is ready to approve (decisions/phases included), `failed` if the run ended without one, or `none` if no run has started in this process.

post.description: Requests a cooperative stop of the in-progress architecture session. The planner loop checks the flag between turns, so cancellation takes effect after the current turn finishes. Any decisions and phases already proposed are auto-finalized to the approval gate; if nothing usable was produced the session ends as `plan.architecture_failed`.

404.description: No architecture session is currently running.

post.description: Launches the autonomous phase-review planner in a background thread while the plan is in `phase_review` status. The planner records lessons and proposes the next phase; the session emits `plan.phase_review_completed` / `plan.phase_review_failed` on termination. The operator then calls `POST /plan/approve-phase`.

409.description: A phase-review session is already in progress.

post.description: **Mid-phase plan decision.** Operator selects which architecture decision IDs to apply while the plan is in `architecture` status, triggering goal dispatch for the first phase.

409.description: Plan is not in `architecture` status (e.g. already in `phase_review`). The body reports the current vs expected status.

post.description: **End-of-phase plan review.** Operator greenlights the phase review while the plan is in `phase_review` status, releasing goals for the next phase. Set `approve_next=false` to finish and mark the plan `done`.

409.description: Plan is not in `phase_review` status. The body reports the current vs expected status.

post.description: **Recovery action.** Re-dispatches goals for the active phase that never got created — e.g. when a goal's branch creation failed during approve-architecture, leaving the phase under-populated with no automatic retry. Reloads each missing goal's spec from the completed planner session and dispatches it. Idempotent: already-dispatched goals are skipped.

409.description: Plan is not in `phase_active` status. The body reports the current vs expected status.

post.description: Starts a tactical LLM refinement session for the given message and returns immediately with a session id. The planner may add, update, or remove tasks and goals. Actions stream over SSE as `plan.refinement_action`; the outcome (actions taken, success, error) is available from `GET /plan/sessions/{session_id}` once the status is `done` or `failed`. Optionally scoped via `focused_node_id` / `focused_goal_id`.

get.description: Read the state of any long-running plan session (refinement or discovery). Terminal sessions carry their outcome in `result`.

post.description: Launches the interactive discovery session in a background thread and returns immediately with a session id. Questions are published over SSE as `plan.discovery_question` and are also readable via `GET /plan/discovery/{session_id}`.

409.description: A discovery session is already in progress.

post.description: Submit the user's answer to the session's current question. The next question (or completion) arrives via SSE / the session GET endpoint.

409.description: The session is not waiting for input.

get.description: Read the session's current state: `waiting_input` carries the pending question; `done` carries the final brief in `result.brief`.
Returns all goal aggregates with their tasks and current status.
Fetches the current state of a goal aggregate by its ID.
Returns the ordered history log of state transitions for a goal.

post.description: Force-requeues every FAILED task across all goals.
Force-requeues every FAILED task belonging to the goal.
Operator finalizes a goal that is in `APPROVED` or `MERGED` status. Records the finalization event; does **not** perform any git operations — the GitHub PR merge must happen through the GitHub UI.

409.description: Goal is not in APPROVED or MERGED status, or already finalized.

post.description: Scans all PENDING goals that declared this goal as a dependency and starts any that are now fully unblocked (all prerequisites merged).
Opens a GitHub PR for a goal that has reached `READY_FOR_REVIEW` status. Idempotent — re-calling returns the existing PR number.

409.description: Goal is not in READY_FOR_REVIEW status.

post.summary: Sync Goal PR Status from GitHub
post.description: Polls GitHub for the current PR state and syncs it into the goal aggregate. Only operates on goals in `AWAITING_PR_APPROVAL` or `APPROVED` status.
Applies eligible PR-driven state transitions (e.g. AWAITING_PR_APPROVAL → APPROVED). Should be called after `/pr/sync`.

get.description: Returns a lightweight summary of all task records.

delete.description: Deletes multiple task records, optionally filtered by status. When `filter_statuses` is omitted, **all** tasks are deleted. Valid status values: `created`, `assigned`, `running`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `canceled`, `merged`.

get.description: Returns the persisted agent stdout/stderr + metadata for a finished task.

post.description: Operator override: force-requeue a task regardless of its current status. The retry counter is **not** incremented — this is an explicit operator action. Cannot be applied to tasks in `MERGED` status.

409.description: Task is in MERGED status and cannot be requeued.

delete.description: Permanently removes a single task record. Does **not** clean up git branches or Redis leases — use `POST /project/reset` for a full teardown.

post.description: Triggers the assignment flow for a task: finds an eligible agent, checks dependencies, and transitions the task to `ASSIGNED`. Outcome values: `assigned`, `not_assignable`, `no_eligible_agent`, `deps_not_met`, `not_found`.
Scans all tasks that declared `task_id` as a dependency and dispatches any that are now fully unblocked (all depends_on tasks have succeeded).
Applies the failure-handling policy to a task: requeues if retries remain, cancels if the retry budget is exhausted. Outcome values: `requeued`, `canceled`, `skipped`, `not_found`.

409.description: Task state transition violated domain invariants.

get.description: Returns all agents currently registered in the agent registry.

post.description: Add or update an agent entry in the registry. If an agent with the same `agent_id` already exists it is replaced. `trust_level` valid values: `low`, `medium`, `high`.

put.description: Replace an existing agent entry. The path `agent_id` is authoritative (any `agent_id` in the body is ignored). Returns the updated agent.

404.description: No agent with that id is registered.

delete.description: Remove an agent entry from the registry.

404.description: No agent with that id is registered.

get.description: Returns all capability tags registered for this project.

post.description: Registers a new capability tag (normalized; a malformed tag is rejected). Idempotent — registering an existing tag is a no-op.

delete.description: Removes a capability tag. No-op if the tag is not registered.

get.description: Returns a flattened read-only view of the active `project_spec.yaml`.

404.description: No spec file found. Run `orchestrate init` first.

post.description: Submits a change proposal for the `ProjectSpec`. The change is written atomically to `project_spec.proposed.yaml`. The live spec is **never** mutated directly — an operator must run `orchestrate spec apply` to promote the proposal.

404.description: No active spec to base the proposal on.

422.description: The proposed spec would fail validation.

post.description: Checks whether the submitted artifact text (task description, file path, or dependency declaration) satisfies the active `ProjectSpec` constraints. Returns `passed=true` when no hard violations were found.

get.description: Returns the project name and mode the API is currently scoped to, as resolved from `.orchestrator/config.json` and the environment.

post.description: **Destructive operator action.** Wipes all tasks, Redis leases, and git branches matching the `task-<id>` naming pattern. Set `keep_agents=true` to preserve the agent registry across the reset. Each step is attempted independently; failures are collected in `errors` rather than aborting the remaining steps.

409.description: Reset blocked by a domain invariant.

get.description: Opens a `text/event-stream` connection that delivers real-time domain events to the frontend. Each connection gets its own queue (multiple tabs all receive every event). Events are JSON objects with `type` and `payload` keys. A `: ping` keep-alive comment is sent every 25 s when idle. Reconnect timeout is set to 3 000 ms.
Returns `ok` when the API is up and the container is wired.

ArchitectureStatusResponse.title: ArchitectureStatusResponse
ArchitectureStatusResponse.description:
Reload-resilient readiness of the autonomous architecture session.

The approval gate is offered only when ``state == "completed"``; clients
hydrate proposed decisions/phases from here so a page refresh mid/after the
run keeps the correct state instead of relying on ephemeral SSE buffers.

CapabilityListResponse.title: CapabilityListResponse
CapabilityListResponse.description: The full set of registered capability tags.

GoalRetryResponse.title: GoalRetryResponse
GoalRetryResponse.description: Result of a bulk 'retry failed tasks' action.

PlanConflictResponse.title: PlanConflictResponse
PlanConflictResponse.description:
409 body for plan lifecycle conflicts — tells the client exactly
which transition was attempted and what the plan state actually is.

SessionAccepted.title: SessionAccepted
SessionAccepted.description: Returned with 202 when a long-running session is started.

SessionStatusResponse.title: SessionStatusResponse
SessionStatusResponse.description: Current state of a long-running session.

SpecResponse.title: SpecResponse
SpecResponse.description: Flattened read-only view of the active ProjectSpec.

TaskLogsResponse.title: TaskLogsResponse
TaskLogsResponse.description: Persisted agent console output for a finished task.

filter_statuses.title: Filter Statuses
filter_statuses.description: List of task statuses to target. When omitted, ALL tasks are pruned.

artifact.title: Artifact
artifact.description: The artifact text to validate (e.g. a task description, file path, or dependency declaration).
